fr.recommendations: Enhancing the visibility and access of critical areas on the property may assist in improving responder operations. Clearly marked and unobstructed access to the main building and detached structure could facilitate swift response times. Consideration for improving visibility, such as lighting and signage, may assist in navigating the premises efficiently. A designated staging area or clearly defined paths in the vehicle parking area could support effective maneuvering and ensure that emergency vehicles are not impeded by ongoing activities. Streamlining access with adequate signage at the single entry point may enhance overall response efficiency and ensure rapid identification of travel routes to various structures. Keeping landscaping trimmed near access pathways may further support visibility and movement for responders throughout the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Encouraging separation between scattered trees and clusters could improve defensible space and reduce potential fire pathways. Managing vegetation adjacent to the structure and fence is crucial to minimizing potential fire exposure and enhancing structure protection. Maintaining reduced vegetation near the perimeter while ensuring that the area remains open will support both defensible space and potential suppression efforts. Improving clearance and access at the rear of the property and along any vegetated perimeters can aid firefighter tactics and overall access. Regular management of scattered shrubs and nearby trees, especially in areas near the residence, would support fuel continuity reduction. These actions combined may enhance property preparedness and contribute to long-term wildfire resilience.
